releaseNotes: v0.38.3 — Encryption at rest, one-tap recall-to-quarantine, and reliability hardening. New: your RxRescue database on Windows is now encrypted at rest (AES-256 / SQLCipher), matching the mobile app — the first launch after updating migrates your existing data automatically, backed up first and revert-safe, so your inventory is never at risk. Recalls now quarantine all matching stock in one tap, and you get a real-time alert if you scan recalled stock during a zone audit. Daily Intake survives an app restart and shows each row's receive date. At a zone audit's finish, the still-missing items are grouped by drug and you can retire or quarantine them on the spot. Edit a bottle's PAR level and demand inline, open a new product-catalog editor from Reports, and new installs get a short first-run welcome. Under the hood: sync-correctness hardening, server-side privacy screening on synced fields, and broader test coverage. The auto-updater rolls 0.38.2 -> 0.38.3 on next launch; your data is unchanged (on Windows it is migrated into encrypted storage, backed up first). 869 tests passing; three-reviewer pre-release audit, all findings fixed.
